intro.description: Quan ho Bac Ninh is a duet singing art between men and women, popular in the ancient Kinh Bac region. In 2009, UNESCO recognized Quan ho as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity, affirming the unique cultural value of the Vietnamese nation.
intro.desc_p1: Quan ho Bac Ninh is a ritual singing practice between groups of male (liền anh) and female (liền chị) singers from different villages, traditional in the ancient Kinh Bac region (now Bac Ninh and Bac Giang provinces). It is more than just a folk song genre; it is a whole system of customs, traditions, and rituals associated with the spiritual life of the local people.
intro.desc_p2: Quan ho has over 300 ancient melodies, each carrying a different emotional shade — from greetings and meetings to partings and farewells. The art of singing Quan ho requires sophisticated vocal techniques like trills, vibrations, and glottal stops — creating a unique timbre that is unmistakable.
intro.unesco_desc: On September 30, 2009, at the session of the UNESCO Intergovernmental Committee in Abu Dhabi, Quan ho Bac Ninh was officially recognized as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. This is a source of great pride for the people of Bac Ninh and the Vietnamese nation as a whole.

timeline.t1_text: Quan ho began to form in the Kinh Bac region, associated with village life and common worship of tutelary deities.
timeline.t2_text: The most brilliant period of development, 49 ancient Quan ho villages were established, forming unique cultural activities.
timeline.t3_text: Quan ho was gradually recorded, researched and became a typical cultural symbol of Bac Ninh - Bac Giang region.
timeline.t4_text: UNESCO officially recognized Quan ho Bac Ninh as the Representative Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.

intro.identity_desc: Discover the unique features that make up the soul of Quan Ho Bac Ninh folk singing.
intro.id_singing_desc: A responsive singing style between men and women with 4 basic techniques: 'Resonant, Ringing, Grounded, Bouncing', demanding sophistication and mastery.
intro.id_custom_desc: A sacred bond between two Quan Ho villages, whose members take an oath never to marry each other.
intro.id_manner_desc: A respectful, elegant way of addressing: calling each other 'người ơi', referring to oneself as 'vang', 'thưa', displaying profound humility.
intro.costume_desc: Every set of costume is a work of art, crystallizing grace and elegance.
intro.female_costume_desc: Standing out with the radiant seven-layered dress, crow-beak scarf, traditional flat palm hat (nón quai thao), and a peach silk sash.
intro.male_costume_desc: Carrying the essence of Kinh Bac gentlemen with black gauze tunic, silk trousers, turban, holding a black umbrella or paper fan.
intro.betel_desc: 'When guests come, if not tea, it is betel'. The phoenix-winged betel is not just a greeting ritual but also shows the host's skill and affection.

footer.description: Preserving and promoting the values of Quan ho Bac Ninh intangible cultural heritage - UNESCO 2009.
